meaning: n.堆积物,沉淀物,存款,押金,保证金,存放物vt.存放,堆积vi.沉淀
examples: A 10% deposit is payable in advance.
Landlords can charge a returnable deposit.
A returnable deposit is payable on arrival.
The party lost its deposit, having secured less than 5% of the total votes cast.
If you trade on advance information about a copper deposit, you are clearly guilty.
